parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a seminar about how employers could improve their employees' work-life balance. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea would be more effective, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: How could employers improve their employees' work-life balance?
stimulus.body:
• allowing genuinely flexible working hours
• guaranteeing a 'right to disconnect' outside working hours
• offering wellbeing support such as counselling and exercise facilities

Some opinions expressed in the seminar:
"Flexibility is meaningless if your manager still expects you at every meeting."
"One late-night email from the boss undoes a week of good intentions."
"A free gym membership does not fix a workload that never ends."

bullets: explain which would be more effective
give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: The directors of the company where you work have invited staff to submit proposals on flexible working.

stimulus.title: Proposal: a flexible working scheme
stimulus.body: Write a proposal recommending a flexible working scheme for your company. Describe the problems the current fixed timetable causes for staff, outline how your scheme would operate, and explain why it would benefit the company as well as its employees.

bullets: the problems caused by the current timetable
the benefits for both staff and the company

tasks.lead: The manager of the organisation where you work part-time is concerned about staff regularly working late, and has asked you to write a report.

stimulus.title: Report: the culture of working late
stimulus.body: Write a report describing how widespread late working has become in your workplace, analysing its effects on staff performance and morale, and recommending two practical measures the manager should introduce.

bullets: how widespread late working has become
its effects on performance and morale

tasks.lead: You have received this letter from an English-speaking friend, Alex.

stimulus.body:
I've been offered a promotion, but everyone in that team seems to work evenings and weekends. The salary is much better, and saying no might look like I lack ambition. But honestly, I love having time for my music and my friends.

You always give me sensible advice - what do you think I should do?

Best wishes,
Alex

bullets: your honest advice on whether to accept the promotion
how Alex could protect their free time if they accept
